extension.description: Debug SQLite/Drift databases in VS Code with Saropa Drift Advisor.
view.databaseExplorer.name: Database
view.pendingChanges.name: Pending Changes
view.toolbox.name: Drift Tools
view.queryPerformance.name: Drift Queries
viewsWelcome.0.contents:
Saropa Drift Advisor is disabled.

Turn on the extension to discover and connect to Drift debug servers.

[Open Settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22driftViewer.enabled%22%5D)
viewsWelcome.2.contents:
No Drift debug server connected.

GET STARTED — Add the Drift Advisor package to your project, then run your app with the debug server.

[Add Saropa Drift Advisor](command:driftViewer.addPackageToProject)

[Open in Browser](command:driftViewer.openInBrowser)
[Troubleshooting](command:driftViewer.showTroubleshooting)
[Connection log](command:driftViewer.showConnectionLog)
[Diagnose connection](command:driftViewer.diagnoseConnection)

[Retry Connection](command:driftViewer.retryDiscovery)
[Refresh sidebar UI](command:driftViewer.refreshConnectionUi)
[Forward Port (Android Emulator)](command:driftViewer.forwardPortAndroid)
[Select Server](command:driftViewer.selectServer)

RESOURCES

[Getting Started Guide](https://github.com/saropa/saropa_drift_advisor#getting-started) • [Report Issue](https://github.com/saropa/saropa_drift_advisor/issues)
viewsWelcome.3.contents:
No Drift debug server connected.

Run your app with the Drift debug server to connect.

[Open in Browser](command:driftViewer.openInBrowser)
[Troubleshooting](command:driftViewer.showTroubleshooting)
[Connection log](command:driftViewer.showConnectionLog)
[Diagnose connection](command:driftViewer.diagnoseConnection)

[Retry Connection](command:driftViewer.retryDiscovery)
[Refresh sidebar UI](command:driftViewer.refreshConnectionUi)
[Forward Port (Android Emulator)](command:driftViewer.forwardPortAndroid)
[Select Server](command:driftViewer.selectServer)

RESOURCES

[Getting Started Guide](https://github.com/saropa/saropa_drift_advisor#getting-started) • [Report Issue](https://github.com/saropa/saropa_drift_advisor/issues)
viewsWelcome.4.contents:
The sidebar reports a connection (HTTP and/or VM Service), but the **Database** tree could not load schema from the REST API. The embedded browser can still work if it bypasses the same path.

Check `driftViewer.authToken`, host/port, VPN/WSL, and that **Select Server** points at the live process.

[Refresh tree](command:driftViewer.refreshTree)
[Diagnose connection](command:driftViewer.diagnoseConnection)
[Troubleshooting](command:driftViewer.showTroubleshooting)
[Connection log](command:driftViewer.showConnectionLog)
[Open in Browser](command:driftViewer.openInBrowser)
[Select Server](command:driftViewer.selectServer)
[Connection help (web)](command:driftViewer.openConnectionHelp)

config.enabled.description: Master switch: when false, the extension does not discover or connect to Drift debug servers and all Drift Advisor features are off.
config.enableMonitoringAndLogging.descri: Global kill switch: when false, silences all diagnostics, stops background sweeps, clears file badges, and instructs the Dart debug server to halt all query recording and timeline captures (its data endpoints answer 403 until resumed).
command.monitoring.kill.title: Kill All Monitoring and Logging
command.monitoring.resume.title: Resume All Monitoring and Logging
config.branching.maxBranches.description: Maximum number of data branches to keep. When exceeded, the oldest branches are pruned first.
config.branching.maxRowsPerTable.descrip: Maximum rows captured per table when creating a data branch. Tables larger than this are captured up to the cap and flagged as truncated.
config.authToken.description: Bearer token for authenticating with the Drift debug server.
config.port.description: Port of the Drift debug server.
config.host.description: Host of the Drift debug server.
config.connection.logEveryUiRefresh.desc: Log every connection UI refresh even when state is unchanged (verbose; for deep troubleshooting).
config.discovery.enabled.description: Automatically scan for running Drift debug servers.
config.logVerbosity.description: Controls how much runtime information the extension writes to Output → Saropa Drift Advisor channels.
config.discovery.portRangeStart.descript: Start of port range to scan for Drift debug servers.
config.discovery.portRangeEnd.descriptio: End of port range to scan for Drift debug servers (inclusive).
config.database.loadOnConnect.descriptio: When true, load the Database tree as soon as a server connects. When false, load on first time the Database view is shown.
config.database.allowOfflineSchema.descr: When the debug server is unreachable, repopulate the Database tree from the last-known persisted schema for this workspace when available.
config.dartSchemaScan.openOutput.descrip: When running **Scan Dart schema definitions**, open the Drift Dart schema output channel automatically.
config.schemaCache.ttlMs.description: Schema cache TTL in ms. Cached schema is reused for this long before refetch. 0 disables in-memory cache.
config.schemaCache.persistKey.descriptio: Workspace state key for last-known schema (stale-while-revalidate on startup). Empty disables persist.
config.lightweight.description: When true, disables file badges, timeline auto-capture, and tree refresh on generation change to reduce load.
config.tasks.blockOnWarnings.description: Block app launch when health check finds warnings (not just errors).
config.performance.slowThresholdMs.descr: Queries slower than this (ms) are highlighted as slow.
config.performance.refreshIntervalMs.des: How often to refresh performance data during debug (ms).
config.performance.logToCapture.descript: Log queries to Saropa Log Capture: off, slow queries only, or all queries.
config.integrations.includeInLogCaptureS: How much to send to Saropa Log Capture at session end: none, header only, or full (header + meta + sidecar).
config.perfRegression.enabled.descriptio: Detect query performance regressions when a debug session ends.
config.perfRegression.threshold.descript: Regression alert threshold: current avg / baseline avg. E.g. 2.0 means 2x slower.
config.perfRegression.recordBaselinesFro: When the Query Replay DVR panel loads or refreshes, merge each timeline row's duration into the perf baseline store (same normalized SQL keys as session baselines).
config.perfRegression.warnOnDvrPanelRefr: When true, each DVR panel refresh compares DVR timings to baselines and may show a regression warning (off by default — can be noisy).
config.fileBadges.enabled.description: Show row count badges on Drift table files in the explorer.
config.hover.enabled.description: Show database row preview on hover during debug sessions.
config.hover.maxRows.description: Number of recent rows to show in hover preview.
config.linter.enabled.description: Show database diagnostics on Drift table definitions.
config.linter.anomalySeverity.descriptio: Severity level for data anomaly diagnostics.
config.timeline.maxSnapshots.description: Maximum number of snapshots to keep in memory.
config.timeline.autoCapture.description: Automatically capture a timeline snapshot whenever database data changes. Off by default so the automatic re-dump of every table on each change is opt-in; it is safe on any schema, including image/attachment BLOB tables, because the capture reads each blob's length, not its bytes. Enable this for continuous snapshots, or capture on demand with the Capture Snapshot command.
config.timeline.minIntervalMs.descriptio: Minimum milliseconds between automatic snapshot captures.
config.timeline.captureDebounceMs.descri: Quiet-period (milliseconds) used to coalesce a burst of database writes into a single snapshot re-dump. Writes arriving within this window reset the timer, so one full physical-table re-scan runs after the writes settle rather than one per write.
config.watch.notifications.description: Show desktop notifications when watched data changes.
config.watch.maxWatchers.description: Maximum number of simultaneous watch queries.
config.dataBreakpoints.enabled.descripti: Enable data breakpoint evaluation during debug sessions.
config.fkHyperlinks.enabled.description: Show foreign key values as clickable links in table data views.
config.impact.maxDepth.description: Maximum FK traversal depth for row impact analysis.
config.impact.maxRowsPerTable.descriptio: Maximum rows to fetch per table branch in impact analysis.
config.seeder.defaultRowCount.descriptio: Default number of rows to generate per table.
config.seeder.nullProbability.descriptio: Probability of generating NULL for nullable columns (0.0-1.0).
config.sqlNotebook.maxHistory.descriptio: Maximum number of SQL queries to keep in history.
config.nlSql.apiUrl.description: LLM API endpoint for NL-to-SQL (OpenAI-compatible chat completions).
config.nlSql.model.description: LLM model used for NL-to-SQL generation.
config.nlSql.maxTokens.description: Maximum response tokens for NL-to-SQL generation.
config.nlSql.seedSuggestions.description: When true, NL-to-SQL shows a QuickPick of past questions, saved filters, and notebook SQL history before the LLM runs.
config.nlSql.maxSchemaTables.description: Max tables included in the NL-to-SQL schema prompt; extra tables are listed as omitted to control token size.
config.nlSql.maxSchemaContextChars.descr: Hard cap on total schema context characters sent to the LLM; the builder truncates with a trailing notice when exceeded.
config.invariants.continuous.description: Re-evaluate invariants on every database change (may impact performance).
config.invariants.defaultSeverity.descri: Default severity level for new invariant violations.
config.report.defaultMaxRows.description: Default maximum rows per table in exported portable reports.
config.report.includeSchema.description: Include schema SQL (CREATE TABLE statements) in exported reports.
config.report.includeAnomalies.descripti: Include anomaly report in exported reports.
config.diagnostics.tableExclusions.descr: Suppress specific diagnostic rules on specific tables. Keys are rule codes, values are arrays of SQL table names.
config.diagnostics.columnExclusions.desc: Suppress specific diagnostic rules on specific columns. Keys are rule codes, values are arrays of 'table.column' identifiers (e.g. 'users.middle_name' for a column expected to be mostly NULL).
config.diagnostics.userDataTables.descri: SQL table names whose live debug rows are not representative of production data (user/demo tables, or static reference tables that load lazily/partially in a debug session). Null-rate and unused-column analysis is skipped for these, since a null rate measured on a partial table is meaningless.
config.logVerbosity.description: How much detail the Saropa Drift Advisor writes to its Output channels: 'quiet' (errors and important events only), 'normal', or 'verbose' (everything).
config.diagnostics.disabledRules.descrip: Diagnostic rule codes to mute everywhere (e.g. 'no-primary-key'). Toggled by clicking a rule in the Drift Advisor Rules sidebar; can also be edited by hand.
config.diagnostics.severityOverrides.des: Override the severity of specific diagnostic rules. Keys are rule codes, values are 'error', 'warning', 'info', or 'hint'.
config.dashboard.showOnConnect.descripti: Show a notification to open the Dashboard when a server first connects.
config.dvr.autoRecord.description: Automatically start Query Replay DVR recording when a Dart/Flutter debug session begins.
config.dvr.maxQueries.description: Maximum number of DVR query events retained by the server ring buffer.
config.dvr.captureBeforeAfter.descriptio: Request before/after state capture for write queries when supported by the server.
walkthrough.driftViewer.gettingStarted.t: Get Started with Saropa Drift Advisor
walkthrough.driftViewer.gettingStarted.d: Learn how to connect to your Flutter/Drift app and explore your SQLite database.
walkthrough.driftViewer.gettingStarted.s:
Add `saropa_drift_advisor` to your Flutter project's dependencies. This enables the debug server that the extension connects to.

[Add Saropa Drift Advisor](command:driftViewer.addPackageToProject)
Adding the drift advisor package to your project

Start your Flutter app in debug mode. The extension auto-discovers Drift debug servers, or connects via VM Service during debugging.

Check the status bar for connection state. If offline, click it to retry.

[Retry Discovery](command:driftViewer.retryDiscovery)
[Select Server](command:driftViewer.selectServer)

Open the Database Explorer in the sidebar to see your tables, columns, and foreign keys. Click any table to view its data.

[Open Database Explorer](command:driftViewer.databaseExplorer.focus)

Get an instant assessment of your database's health — index coverage, FK integrity, null density, query performance, and more.

[Run Health Check](command:driftViewer.healthScore)

Diff your Dart code schema against the runtime database and generate migration code automatically.

[Schema Diff](command:driftViewer.schemaDiff)
[Generate Migration](command:driftViewer.generateMigration)
